$description: BIM TILE SYSTEM token category — the Key Plans and Tiles assembly taxonomy. A Tile is a composable floor plate segment that combines Private Office, Professional Office, and Corporate Office key plans into standardised building-width increments. Two standard Tile sizes: Small (2,700 SF / 250.84 m²) and Large (4,900 SF / 455.22 m²). Tiles compose to fill the 20,000 SF net leasable floor plate. Design principle: maximise climate zone autonomy (one Tile = one HVAC zone) while maintaining rolling efficiency (ability to reach 100% utilisation as tenant mix changes). Source: AEC_Floor Plates_Tiles_Alternatives.pdf (V12, 2025-01-07, Collaborators #27); AEC_Floor Plates_Methodology_V12.pdf (Collaborators #27). See also floor-plate-standards.dtcg.json for floor plate size hierarchy.

small-tile.$description: Small Tile family — 2,700 SF (250.84 m²) per tile. Seven tile types covering the full range of corporate, private, and professional office configurations at the 2,700 SF increment.

$value.label: Tile A — Corporate Office
$value.notes: Single corporate tenant unit. Maximum climate zone autonomy for corporate tenant. Simplest tile composition.

tile-a.$description: Tile A: Corporate Office — 2,700 SF single unit. One climate zone. Simplest tile composition.

$value.label: Tile B-1 — Private Office End Cap
$value.notes: Five private licensed offices plus corridor. End Cap tile — placed at building short-side or corner. Each private office is an independent climate zone. Total 1,800 SF office + 900 SF corridor.

tile-b1.$description: Tile B-1: Private Office End Cap — 2,700 SF total; 5 private offices (300 + 450 + 300 + 450 + 300 SF) + 900 SF corridor. End Cap placement.

$value.label: Tile C-1 — Professional Office (Medium + Small)
$value.notes: Medium Professional Office combined with Small Professional Office. Standard mid-floor tile for professional centres.

tile-c1.$description: Tile C-1: Professional Office — 2,700 SF; Medium (2,000 SF) + Small (700 SF) combination. 2 climate zones.

$value.label: Tile C-2 — Professional Office (Medium Variant)
$value.notes: Two medium Professional Office units side-by-side. Flexible for medical or business sub-types.

tile-c2.$description: Tile C-2: Professional Office — 2,700 SF; two Medium (1,100 SF each) units + 500 SF corridor. 2 climate zones.

$value.label: Tile C-3 — Professional Office (Large + Small)
$value.notes: Large Professional Office with a small private office attached. Suitable for laboratory or medical anchor tenant with small adjacent unit.

tile-c3.$description: Tile C-3: Professional Office — 2,700 SF; Large (2,400 SF) + Small (300 SF). 2 climate zones.

$value.label: Tile C-4 — Professional Office (Corridor Variant)
$value.notes: Single larger Professional Office with integrated corridor and support. Suitable for civic or academic sub-types requiring more contiguous space.

tile-c4.$description: Tile C-4: Professional Office — 2,700 SF; single larger unit (2,200 SF) + corridor/support (500 SF). 1 climate zone.

$value.label: Tile E-1 — Mixed End Cap (Left)
$value.notes: Mixed Private and Professional end cap for left side of building short face. Handles corner column grid resolution.

tile-e1.$description: Tile E-1: Mixed End Cap Left — 2,700 SF; Private offices + Professional Office Small + corridor. Left building end.

$value.label: Tile E-2 — Mixed End Cap (Right)
$value.notes: Mirror image of Tile E-1 for right side of building short face. Small tiles at building corners may create boxing issues — review structural grid before specifying.

tile-e2.$description: Tile E-2: Mixed End Cap Right — 2,700 SF; mirror of E-1 for right building end.

large-tile.$description: Large Tile family — 4,900 SF (455.22 m²) per tile. Three tile types for large corporate and professional configurations. Large tiles reduce the number of climate zones but offer more contiguous space per tenant.

$value.label: Tile F — Large Corporate Office
$value.notes: Single large corporate tenant unit. Nearly a 1/4 floor tile. Maximum contiguous space for single-tenant corporate use. One climate zone.

tile-f.$description: Tile F: Large Corporate Office — 4,900 SF single unit. 1 climate zone. Approaches 1/4 floor tile at 5,000 SF.

$value.label: Tile G — Large Private Office Mix
$value.notes: Ten private licensed offices (averaging 300–500 SF each) plus corridor. Maximum climate zone count per tile — each private office is fully independently climate-controlled. High density of small tenants.

tile-g.$description: Tile G: Large Private Office Mix — 4,900 SF; 10 private office units (300–500 SF each) + corridor. 10 climate zones.

$value.label: Tile H — Large Professional Office
$value.notes: Three Professional Office units (any mix of Medical, Business, Laboratory, Academic, Civic sub-types) plus corridor. Spans the full range of Professional Office sub-types. Suitable for mixed-professional-centre floors.

tile-h.$description: Tile H: Large Professional Office — 4,900 SF; 3 Professional Office units (any sub-type mix) + corridor. 3 climate zones.

design-principles.$description: Encoded design principles for the Key Plans and Tiles assembly system. These are not measurable tokens but captured as reference metadata for the BIM design system substrate.

climate-zone-autonomy.$description: Each Tile is an independent HVAC climate zone. Tenants seeking smaller tiles are motivated by climate control autonomy, not just space size. Combining tiles means combining climate control — negotiate accordingly.

rolling-efficiency.$description: The Tile composition must allow 100% of the floor plate to be represented by Key Plans at any tenant mix. No free space should exist without a corresponding Key Plan. Goal: reach 100% utilisation as tenant mix evolves.

corner-handling.$description: Building short sides and corners use End Cap tiles (B-1, E-1, E-2). Small tiles at corners may create structural-grid boxing issues. Review column grid before specifying corner tile types. Core position should be adjusted if short-side tiles conflict.

professional-to-corporate-jump.$description: The jump from Professional Office (max ~5,000 SF per 1/4 tile) to Corporate Office (min 2,500 SF per 1/8 tile) may leave a gap for tenants needing 5,000–7,500 SF. Consider Tile H (4,900 SF) or Small + Small Professional combinations (4,198 SF) to bridge the gap.
